title: Syzygium gageanum
intro: Syzygium gageanum, also known as the Indian olive, is a small tree species native to the Western Ghats of India, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium gageanum include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Syzygium gageanum is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is a member of the Syzygium genus, a group of plants native to tropical Asia. The Myrtaceae family includes over 3,000 species of flowering plants, with many species found in Australia, New Zealand, and parts of Asia and the Pacific. Syzygium gageanum is often found growing as an understorey tree in tropical forests. This plant is classified as a species of flowering plant within the broader taxonomy of the Syzygium genus and the Myrtaceae family.
